---
title: "Setup Guide - Spring Boot Task Management System"
author: "Armankarami"
date: "2025-10-13"
format: html
toc: true
toc-depth: 3
---

# Setup Guide

## Prerequisites

### Required Software

| Software | Version | Description | Download Link |
|----------|---------|-------------|---------------|
| **Java** | 17+ | Java Development Kit | [OpenJDK 17](https://openjdk.org/projects/jdk/17/) |
| **Maven** | 3.9+ | Build Tool | [Apache Maven](https://maven.apache.org/download.cgi) |
| **Git** | 2.0+ | Version Control | [Git](https://git-scm.com/downloads) |

### Optional Software

| Software | Version | Description | Download Link |
|----------|---------|-------------|---------------|
| **IntelliJ IDEA** | 2025.2+ | IDE | [IntelliJ IDEA](https://www.jetbrains.com/idea/) |
| **Postman** | Latest | API Testing | [Postman](https://www.postman.com/downloads/) |
| **curl** | Latest | Command Line Tool | Included in most systems |

## Installation Steps

### 1. Java Installation

#### Windows
```powershell
# Download OpenJDK 17 from https://openjdk.org/projects/jdk/17/
# Install and set JAVA_HOME environment variable
# Add %JAVA_HOME%\bin to PATH

# Verify installation
java -version
javac -version
```

#### macOS
```bash
# Using Homebrew (recommended)
brew install openjdk@17

# Add to PATH
echo 'export PATH="/opt/homebrew/opt/openjdk@17/bin:$PATH"' >> ~/.zshrc
source ~/.zshrc

# Verify installation
java -version
javac -version
```

#### Linux (Ubuntu/Debian)
```bash
# Update package list
sudo apt update

# Install OpenJDK 17
sudo apt install openjdk-17-jdk

# Verify installation
java -version
javac -version
```

### 2. Maven Installation

#### Windows
```powershell
# Download Maven from https://maven.apache.org/download.cgi
# Extract to C:\Program Files\Apache\maven
# Set MAVEN_HOME environment variable
# Add %MAVEN_HOME%\bin to PATH

# Verify installation
mvn -version
```

#### macOS
```bash
# Using Homebrew (recommended)
brew install maven

# Verify installation
mvn -version
```

#### Linux (Ubuntu/Debian)
```bash
# Install Maven
sudo apt install maven

# Verify installation
mvn -version
```

### 3. Project Setup

#### Clone the Repository
```bash
# Clone the project
git clone <repository-url>
cd taskManager

# Verify project structure
ls -la
```

#### Project Structure Verification
```
taskManager/
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ src/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ main/
в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ java/
в”‚   в”‚   в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ com/
в”‚   в”‚   в”‚       в””в”Ђв”Ђ example/
в”‚   в”‚   в”‚           в””в”Ђв”Ђ taskManager/
в”‚   в”‚   в”‚               в”њв”Ђв”Ђ TaskManagementApplication.java
в”‚   в”‚   в”‚               в”њв”Ђв”Ђ TaskController.java
в”‚   в”‚   в”‚               в”њв”Ђв”Ђ controller/
в”‚   в”‚   в”‚               в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ TaskController.java
в”‚   в”‚   в”‚               в”њв”Ђв”Ђ model/
в”‚   в”‚   в”‚               в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ Task.java
в”‚   в”‚   в”‚               в””в”Ђв”Ђ service/
в”‚   в”‚   в”‚                   в””в”Ђв”Ђ TaskService.java
в”‚   в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ resources/
в”‚   в”‚       в””в”Ђв”Ђ application.properties
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ test/
в”‚       в””в”Ђв”Ђ java/
в”‚           в””в”Ђв”Ђ com/
в”‚               в””в”Ђв”Ђ example/
в”‚                   в””в”Ђв”Ђ taskManager/
в”‚                       в”њв”Ђв”Ђ TaskManagementApplicationTests.java
в”‚                       в”њв”Ђв”Ђ controller/
в”‚                       в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ TaskControllerTest.java
в”‚                       в””в”Ђв”Ђ service/
в”‚                           в””в”Ђв”Ђ TaskServiceTest.java
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ target/
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ documentation/
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ pom.xml
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ mvnw
в””в”Ђв”Ђ mvnw.cmd
```

## Building the Project

### 1. Clean and Compile

```bash
# Clean previous builds
./mvnw clean

# Compile the project
./mvnw compile

# Verify compilation
ls target/classes/com/example/taskManager/
```

### 2. Run Tests

```bash
# Run all tests
./mvnw test

# Run specific test class
./mvnw test -Dtest=TaskControllerTest

# Run tests with coverage
./mvnw test jacoco:report
```

### 3. Package the Application

```bash
# Create executable JAR
./mvnw package

# Verify JAR creation
ls target/*.jar
```

## Running the Application

### 1. Development Mode

```bash
# Start the application
./mvnw spring-boot:run

# Application will start on http://localhost:8080
# Press Ctrl+C to stop
```

### 2. Production Mode

```bash
# Run the JAR file
java -jar target/taskManager-0.0.1-SNAPSHOT.jar

# Application will start on http://localhost:8080
# Press Ctrl+C to stop
```

### 3. Background Mode

```bash
# Run in background (Unix/Linux/macOS)
nohup java -jar target/taskManager-0.0.1-SNAPSHOT.jar > app.log 2>&1 &

# Check if running
ps aux | grep java

# Stop the application
pkill -f taskManager
```

## Configuration

### 1. Application Properties

The application uses `src/main/resources/application.properties` for configuration:

```properties
# Server configuration
server.port=8080
server.servlet.context-path=/

# Logging configuration
logging.level.com.example.taskManager=INFO
logging.level.org.springframework.web=INFO

# Application configuration
spring.application.name=taskManager
```

### 2. Environment Variables

You can override configuration using environment variables:

```bash
# Change port
export SERVER_PORT=9090
./mvnw spring-boot:run

# Change logging level
export LOGGING_LEVEL_COM_EXAMPLE_TASKMANAGER=DEBUG
./mvnw spring-boot:run
```

### 3. Profile Configuration

Create profile-specific configuration files:

```properties
# application-dev.properties
server.port=8080
logging.level.com.example.taskManager=DEBUG

# application-prod.properties
server.port=80
logging.level.com.example.taskManager=WARN
```

Run with specific profile:

```bash
./mvnw spring-boot:run -Dspring.profiles.active=dev
```

## IDE Setup

### 1. IntelliJ IDEA

#### Import Project
1. Open IntelliJ IDEA
2. Select "Open" or "Import Project"
3. Navigate to the project directory
4. Select `pom.xml`
5. Choose "Import project from external model" в†’ "Maven"
6. Click "Next" and "Finish"

#### Configure Run Configuration
1. Go to Run в†’ Edit Configurations
2. Click "+" в†’ "Spring Boot"
3. Set:
   - **Name**: `TaskManagementApplication`
   - **Main class**: `com.example.taskManager.TaskManagementApplication`
   - **Module**: `taskManager`
4. Click "OK"

#### Run the Application
1. Select the run configuration
2. Click the "Run" button
3. Application starts on http://localhost:8080

### 2. Eclipse

#### Import Project
1. Open Eclipse
2. File в†’ Import в†’ Existing Maven Projects
3. Select the project directory
4. Click "Finish"

#### Run the Application
1. Right-click on `TaskManagementApplication.java`
2. Run As в†’ Java Application
3. Application starts on http://localhost:8080

### 3. VS Code

#### Install Extensions
- Extension Pack for Java
- Spring Boot Extension Pack

#### Open Project
1. File в†’ Open Folder
2. Select the project directory
3. VS Code will detect Maven project

#### Run the Application
1. Open `TaskManagementApplication.java`
2. Click "Run" above the main method
3. Application starts on http://localhost:8080

## Verification

### 1. Health Check

```bash
# Check if application is running
curl http://localhost:8080/

# Expected response: "Hello World!"
```

### 2. API Endpoints

```bash
# Test all endpoints
curl http://localhost:8080/tasks
curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/tasks \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"title":"Test Task","isDone":false}'
curl http://localhost:8080/tasks/1
curl -X PUT http://localhost:8080/tasks/1 \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"title":"Updated Task","isDone":true}'
curl -X DELETE http://localhost:8080/tasks/1
```

### 3. Logs Verification

Check application logs for:
- Application startup
- Request handling
- Error messages

```bash
# View logs in real-time
tail -f app.log

# Search for errors
grep -i error app.log
```

## Troubleshooting

### Common Issues

#### 1. Port Already in Use

**Error**: `Port 8080 was already in use`

**Solution**:
```bash
# Find process using port 8080
lsof -i :8080

# Kill the process
kill -9 <PID>

# Or use different port
export SERVER_PORT=9090
./mvnw spring-boot:run
```

#### 2. Java Version Mismatch

**Error**: `Unsupported major.minor version`

**Solution**:
```bash
# Check Java version
java -version

# Ensure Java 17+ is installed
# Update JAVA_HOME if necessary
```

#### 3. Maven Build Failures

**Error**: `Failed to execute goal`

**Solution**:
```bash
# Clean and rebuild
./mvnw clean compile

# Update dependencies
./mvnw dependency:resolve

# Check for conflicts
./mvnw dependency:tree
```

#### 4. Test Failures

**Error**: `Tests run: X, Failures: Y`

**Solution**:
```bash
# Run tests with debug info
./mvnw test -X

# Run specific test
./mvnw test -Dtest=TaskControllerTest

# Skip tests
./mvnw package -DskipTests
```

### Performance Issues

#### 1. Slow Startup

**Symptoms**: Application takes long time to start

**Solutions**:
- Increase JVM memory: `-Xmx512m`
- Use JVM flags: `-XX:TieredStopAtLevel=1`
- Profile startup time

#### 2. Memory Issues

**Symptoms**: OutOfMemoryError

**Solutions**:
- Increase heap size: `-Xmx1024m`
- Monitor memory usage
- Check for memory leaks

### Network Issues

#### 1. Connection Refused

**Error**: `Connection refused`

**Solutions**:
- Check if application is running
- Verify port configuration
- Check firewall settings

#### 2. CORS Issues

**Error**: `CORS policy blocked`

**Solutions**:
- Add CORS configuration
- Use same-origin requests
- Configure proxy

## Production Deployment

### 1. Docker Deployment

#### Create Dockerfile
```dockerfile
FROM openjdk:17-jdk-slim
COPY target/taskManager-0.0.1-SNAPSHOT.jar app.jar
EXPOSE 8080
ENTRYPOINT ["java", "-jar", "/app.jar"]
```

#### Build and Run
```bash
# Build Docker image
docker build -t taskManager .

# Run container
docker run -p 8080:8080 taskManager
```

### 2. Systemd Service

#### Create Service File
```ini
[Unit]
Description=TaskManager Application
After=network.target

[Service]
Type=simple
User=app
ExecStart=/usr/bin/java -jar /opt/taskManager/app.jar
Restart=always
RestartSec=10

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
```

#### Enable Service
```bash
# Copy service file
sudo cp taskManager.service /etc/systemd/system/

# Reload systemd
sudo systemctl daemon-reload

# Enable and start service
sudo systemctl enable taskManager
sudo systemctl start taskManager
```

### 3. Cloud Deployment

#### AWS EC2
1. Launch EC2 instance
2. Install Java 17
3. Upload JAR file
4. Configure security groups
5. Start application

#### Google Cloud Platform
1. Create Compute Engine instance
2. Install Java 17
3. Upload JAR file
4. Configure firewall rules
5. Start application

## Monitoring and Maintenance

### 1. Health Checks

```bash
# Application health
curl http://localhost:8080/actuator/health

# Application info
curl http://localhost:8080/actuator/info
```

### 2. Log Management

```bash
# Rotate logs
logrotate /etc/logrotate.d/taskManager

# Monitor logs
tail -f /var/log/taskManager/application.log
```

### 3. Performance Monitoring

```bash
# Monitor JVM
jstat -gc <PID>

# Monitor memory
jmap -histo <PID>

# Monitor threads
jstack <PID>
```

## Next Steps

After successful setup:

1. **Explore the API**: Use Postman or curl to test endpoints
2. **Read Documentation**: Review all documentation files
3. **Run Tests**: Execute the test suite
4. **Modify Code**: Make changes and test
5. **Deploy**: Follow production deployment guide

## Support

For issues and questions:

1. **Check Logs**: Review application logs
2. **Read Documentation**: Consult this guide
3. **Search Issues**: Look for similar problems
4. **Create Issue**: Report bugs or request features

---

*Last updated: 2025-10-13*
